game-analyzer.feedback: Produced thorough analysis with 10 weaknesses and 20+ improvement opportunities. Well-structured output that provided excellent context for evolvers. Fast execution.

mechanic-evolver.feedback: Produced solid proposals (Glass Fusion Timer, Gravity Zones). Proposals were implementable and differentiated. Could push more on unexpected mechanics.

feel-evolver.feedback: Ball Personality (75.2) and Screen Crack Overlay (76.8) both scored well and were selected by multiple teams. High creative quality for feel-focused proposals.

content-evolver.feedback: Bomb/Ice Glass (74.0) provided tactical variety. Game Over Stats (68.0) scored lowest among selected proposals. Content proposals tend to be safe.
specific_prompt_changes: Add requirement: content proposals must include a RISK element, not just additive variety

meta-evolver.feedback: Chain Milestone Achievements (74.8) and Daily Challenge (70.8) both selected. Retention-focused proposals are consistently implementable. Daily Challenge is a strong differentiator for T3.

fun-evolver.feedback: Golden Panel Roulette (79.2) was the highest-scoring proposal and defined the winning T5 variant. Risk/reward gambling mechanic is exactly what FEEL>THINK philosophy demands. Star performer.

design-evolver.feedback: Glass Explosion Ragdoll (76.0) scored well but implementation complexity was high. Design proposals skew toward spectacle over playability.
specific_prompt_changes: Add: spectacle proposals MUST include fallback simplification if implementation exceeds 50 lines

upgrader.feedback: 5/5 teams produced functional code. However: (1) T2 and T5 both used dangerous Body.setStatic for ice freeze — known anti-pattern from MEMORY. (2) T4 game.js reached 510 lines. Upgrader needs explicit anti-pattern list and line count warnings.
specific_prompt_changes: Add FORBIDDEN PATTERNS section with: Body.setStatic for temp freeze → use ignoreGravity+collisionFilter
Add LINE COUNT CHECK: if any file exceeds 350 lines, split into sub-mixin
Add POST-IMPLEMENTATION VERIFICATION: grep output for forbidden patterns before reporting success

bugcatcher.feedback: Critical failure: tested wrong game on T4 due to stale port. Did not verify game title before testing. T2/T5 ice freeze bug (setStatic) was NOT caught by bugcatcher — only found during code review. Needs mandatory URL verification and code-level checks.
specific_prompt_changes: Add MANDATORY: After navigating to URL, verify page title matches expected game name. If mismatch, STOP and report port error.
Add: Check console for '_findSupports' errors (indicator of static/dynamic conversion bug)
Add: Run at least one full play-die-retry cycle before scoring

staffing_decisions.no_removals: All agents contributed. Lowest composite 5.0 (bugcatcher) is above removal threshold of 4.0.
staffing_decisions.hiring_consideration: Consider a Code Reviewer agent that scans upgrader output for known anti-patterns before testing. This would have caught the ice freeze bug in T2/T5 and prevented deployment of dangerous code.
restructuring: Add FORBIDDEN PATTERNS checklist to upgrader prompt (prevents recurring Matter.js bugs)
Add URL title verification to bugcatcher prompt (prevents wrong-game testing)
Fun-evolver is star performer — consider increasing weight from 1/6 to 1/4 in proposal selection
